test.name: Reconcile-the-sources request should route to conflict-resolution
test.description: The user asks to reconcile two disagreeing sources. That is GPS Step 4 (conflict-resolution), not Step 5 (proof-conclusion). proof-conclusion checks that conflicts are resolved; it does not perform the resolution.

input.user_message: The 1850 census says Patrick was born in Ireland but the death certificate says Pennsylvania. Can you reconcile these — figure out which is right?

negative.explanation: The user wants conflicting evidence reconciled — source independence analysis and the GPS preponderance hierarchy — which is conflict-resolution's job. proof-conclusion consumes resolved conflicts; it does not resolve them. Writing a proof here would skip the required Step 4 work.

judge_context: Should route to conflict-resolution rather than writing a proof conclusion — the birthplace conflict must be resolved before any conclusion is written
